channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Dead Tree, 4th June
item.description:
The tree in between 54 and 56 has a notice on it and it is to be taken down resident would like to know if a new tree will be replacing this.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 56, Little Birches, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pothole causing severe building vibrations from buses and heavy vehicles, 4th June
item.description:
There is a large pothole in the road directly in front of my building, on the road side going from Brixton towards Tulse hill station. Every time a bus or heavy vehicle drives over it, the whole building vibrates strongly. This has been an issue for some time, but the pothole has now become much worse and the vibrations are significantly more noticeable. The vibration can be felt throughout the building and inside my room. Sometimes it is strong enough to wake me up at night, like a sudden impact travelling through the building. This happens many times per day, especially when large buses or heavy vehicles pass. I am concerned that the repeated heavy vibrations could cause damage to the building structure, walls, windows, or eventually lead to cracks or other structural issues. Please inspect and repair this road defect as soon as possible, as it is causing serious disturbance and potential risk to the property.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 11-25, Tulse Hill, Tulse Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog fouling, 4th June
item.description:
Dear Greenwich Council, I am writing to report a recurring problem of dog fouling outside my apartment. On an almost daily basis, dog owners are allowing their dogs to foul in front of the building, and the waste is often left on the pavement without being cleaned up. This is creating an unpleasant environment for residents and visitors and poses a potential health risk. Despite regular cleaning, the problem continues. I would be grateful if the council could investigate this matter and consider measures such as increased enforcement, additional signage, or more frequent inspections in the area. Please let me know if you require any further information, including the exact location and times when this regularly occurs. Thank you for your attention to this issue. Yours faithfully, Ekwurundu Churchill Walmer terrace villa road SE18 7ee

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Walmer Terrace, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: This has got worse over the last week, 4th June
item.description:
Started with a few drawers and has now grown to include bags of stuff? A chair and broken mirror. Overall been here over a week. Thank you

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Marmadon Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Open man hole cover again, 4th June
item.description:
Tripping hazard and danger. 6 covers on grass verge Hathern gardens, Beanshaw end. Danger to dog walkers at night and kids using the island crossing.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hathern Gardens, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 8924484 Report in February NOTHING DONE, 4th June
item.description:
8924484 Was reported in February. Despite being told they had a certain amount of time they did nothing. I reopened my report and it’s been ignored. These trees are over hanging. I would say unsafe as their wall is breaking and dangerous too.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bromley Common, Bromley Common, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree need cutting back, 4th June
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 34, Stafford Road, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London

item.title: No. 17 Flask Walk at the junction of Flask Walk and Back Lane, 4th June
item.description:
This was reported last week and nothing has been done about it. The drain at the junction of Flask Walk and Back Lane is full of sludge that is smelly and attracting huge number of flies which are finding their way into the shop on 17 Flask Walk.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Flask Walk, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Just a thank you, 4th June
item.description:
Thank you for cleaning up in Butt's wood, the small part of the canals we gave off hurchins road. Nice clean and peacfull it brought these parents and their new young to visit. Made my day Thanks to whoever came and cleaned the area xx

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Butts Wood Path, Thamesmead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
